meaning: adv.荳咲撫郛ｩ蝨ｰ,荳榊ｦ･蜊丞慍
examples: They were unflinchingly loyal to their friends.
He held out his hand unflinchingly for the cane.
They went to their death unflinchingly, worthy of the title of heroes.
It has servants who are unflinchingly stoical and masters whose self-restraint deserts them.
She unflinchingly grabbed Pippin's crown, put it on and danced with the aplomb that is honed with years of experience.
